data.title: FAQ | Registration, Licensing & Compliance Questions

hero.title: Frequently asked questions
hero.description: Practical answers to the questions organisations ask most often about registration, compliance, and working with Exxonim.

items.question: What types of entities can Exxonim help register?
items.answer: Exxonim supports registration for companies, NGOs, and business names. The team guides you through entity type selection, document preparation, and authority submission.

items.question: How long does company registration take?
items.answer: Timelines depend on the entity type and authority processing speed. Exxonim tracks every submission and follows up proactively so you always know where things stand.

items.question: What is a TIN and do I need one?
items.answer: A Tax Identification Number is required for most registered entities in Tanzania. Exxonim handles TIN applications as part of the setup process.

items.question: Can I track the status of my consultation?
items.answer: Yes. Every consultation is assigned a reference ID. You can use it to check what is complete, what is pending, and what comes next at every stage.

items.question: What happens after I submit a request?
items.answer: The Exxonim team reviews your case, confirms the required documents, and prepares the filing sequence. You receive a reference ID to track progress throughout.

items.question: Does Exxonim handle licensing renewals?
items.answer: Yes. Exxonim tracks renewal deadlines, prepares the required documents, and submits renewals on your behalf so obligations do not lapse.

data.metaDescription: Answers to common questions about business registration, TIN applications, licensing renewals, and consultation tracking with Exxonim Consult in Tanzania.
